backend developer:
Backend Engineer

We are building a Python-first service platform and need an engineer who can own REST APIs, data pipelines, and the infrastructure that keeps them healthy in production.

What you will do
• Design and implement REST APIs: resource modelling, versioning, error contracts, and auth (OAuth 2.0 / JWT).
• Own the PostgreSQL data layer — schema design, migrations, query optimisation, and indexing for read-heavy paths.
• Use Redis for caching hot data and managing background job queues (rate limiting, retry budgets, deduplication).
• Write automated tests at unit and integration level; participate in code review and on-call rotation.
• Ship changes through GitHub Actions CI/CD to AWS; maintain runbooks and alert thresholds for your services.

What we look for
• 4+ years building production backend services.
• Strong Python or Java (we are primarily Python; Java candidates expected to ramp quickly).
• Expert-level SQL and solid understanding of relational data modelling.
• Hands-on Docker and Linux; experience shipping through a CI/CD pipeline with a team.

Nice to have
• GraphQL schema design for BFF or API-product patterns.
• MongoDB or another document store from a polyglot persistence setup.
• Kafka or RabbitMQ for high-throughput event streaming.
• Terraform or AWS CDK for infrastructure provisioning.
• gRPC experience in service-to-service communication.
business analyst:
Business Analyst

You will bridge business stakeholders and delivery teams on a digitisation or systems-integration programme, turning goals into documented, traceable requirements that survive UAT and audit.

What you will do
• Facilitate discovery workshops and produce process maps, user stories, and acceptance criteria aligned to BRD and FRD standards.
• Maintain requirements and decisions in Jira and Confluence; manage traceability from requirement through test case to sign-off.
• Plan and support UAT cycles: write test cases, coordinate testers, triage defects, and document outcomes.
• Perform gap analysis between as-is and to-be processes; use SQL and Excel to validate data and support impact assessments.
• Manage scope through change requests; communicate status and risks to project managers and business owners.

What we look for
• 4+ years as a business analyst on IT or software delivery programmes.
• Strong facilitation, written communication, and stakeholder management across technical and non-technical audiences.
• Proficiency in SQL for data queries and Excel for analysis; confident with Jira and Confluence.
• Solid agile or waterfall delivery experience; understanding of SDLC phases.

Nice to have
• Domain exposure in BFSI, insurance, or enterprise ERP programmes.
• Familiarity with SAP, Salesforce, or ServiceNow from a requirements or UAT perspective.
• Experience with API contracts or data integration design.
customer success manager:
Customer Success Manager — B2B SaaS

You will manage a portfolio of commercial accounts post-sale: structured onboarding, adoption coaching, renewal ownership, and escalation management. Success is measured on product adoption, health scores, NPS, and gross retention.

What you will do
• Lead onboarding and training for new accounts; define success plans with agreed milestones and owners.
• Run quarterly business reviews using product usage data, support trends, and roadmap alignment.
• Own renewal timelines; collaborate with the account executive on expansion opportunities and at-risk signals.
• Triage escalations with support and engineering; track commitments and close the loop with customers.
• Maintain accurate account health data and activity notes in Salesforce.

What we look for
• 3+ years in customer success, account management, or solutions consulting for a software product.
• Clear written and verbal communication; comfortable presenting to director and VP-level stakeholders.
• Data-driven approach: you use usage metrics, NPS, and CSAT to guide conversations.
• Organised execution across a portfolio of 30–80 accounts simultaneously.

Nice to have
• Salesforce admin-level familiarity for pipeline and health-score tracking.
• Experience with product-led growth motions or enterprise procurement cycles.
• Exposure to CS platforms such as Gainsight or Totango.
data analyst:
Data Analyst — BI and Analytics

You will own reporting and exploratory analysis for product and growth teams: trusted metrics, self-serve dashboards, and clear narratives that support decisions at every level.

What you will do
• Write advanced SQL against Snowflake; build and document reliable transformations so downstream consumers can self-serve.
• Design and iterate dashboards in Tableau that balance depth for analysts with clarity for executives.
• Support A/B tests and experiments: define metrics, validate instrumentation, sanity-check results, and communicate findings.
• Perform ad hoc investigations — funnel diagnostics, cohort analyses, anomaly triage — and turn raw findings into actionable recommendations.
• Document metric definitions, edge cases, and known data quality issues so the org builds trust in numbers.

What we look for
• 2+ years in analytics, BI, or a data-heavy operations role.
• Advanced SQL (window functions, CTEs, query optimisation) as your primary tool.
• Experience building dashboards in Tableau or Power BI that non-analysts use daily.
• Genuine curiosity about what a metric means; comfort saying when it is poorly defined.

Nice to have
• Python with pandas for analysis that goes beyond SQL.
• BigQuery or Redshift experience if transitioning from a GCP or AWS data stack.
• dbt or equivalent for versioned, tested transformations.
• Looker or Metabase familiarity.
data engineer:
Data Engineer — Pipelines and Warehouse

You will build and operate batch pipelines and the warehouse layer that powers analytics, reporting, and ML features. We run Snowflake as our primary warehouse with Airflow for orchestration and dbt for transformations.

What you will do
• Implement ELT pipelines in Python that land raw data into Snowflake from application databases, third-party APIs, and cloud storage on AWS S3.
• Write and maintain dbt models: tested, documented transformations with clear ownership and data contracts with producing teams.
• Orchestrate jobs in Airflow; manage SLAs, backfills, and failure alerting without over-engineering dependencies.
• Monitor data quality and lineage; resolve schema changes, upstream incidents, and volume anomalies with analysts and engineers.
• Apply AWS IAM, encryption at rest, and access control patterns appropriate to sensitive data.

What we look for
• 3+ years as a data engineer in production.
• Strong Python and expert SQL; proven Snowflake or Redshift experience.
• Hands-on dbt and Airflow in a team setting.
• AWS fundamentals (S3, Glue, Lambda, or equivalents) and cloud cost awareness.

Nice to have
• BigQuery if coming from a GCP environment.
• Apache Spark for large-scale batch processing beyond Snowflake compute.
• Kafka or Kinesis for streaming ingestion into Snowpipe.
• Fivetran, Airbyte, or Informatica for managed connector use cases.
data scientist:
Data Scientist — Applied Machine Learning

You will develop models and analyses that directly change product decisions: ranking, propensity scoring, demand forecasting, or NLP features depending on the roadmap. Work spans offline experimentation, rigorous measurement, and handoff to engineering.

What you will do
• Frame problems with product and engineering; choose appropriate supervised or unsupervised approaches; validate with held-out sets and error analysis.
• Build models in Python using scikit-learn, XGBoost, or LightGBM for classical approaches; PyTorch or TensorFlow for deep learning where the problem warrants it.
• Write SQL to generate features, run backtests, and investigate production data quality.
• Design and analyse A/B tests; communicate statistical uncertainty and practical significance to non-technical stakeholders.
• Document assumptions, feature definitions, model cards, and known failure modes for peer review.

What we look for
• Masters or PhD in a quantitative discipline, or a Bachelors with strong applied ML experience and shipped or deeply influenced deployed models.
• Solid statistics and classical ML fundamentals.
• Advanced SQL and Python (pandas, numpy, scikit-learn) as daily tools.

Nice to have
• MLOps: feature stores, model registries, drift monitoring.
• NLP or computer vision applied at scale.
• R for statistical modelling or academic publishing context.
devops engineer:
DevOps / Platform Engineer

You will raise the reliability and developer productivity of our AWS-based platform: paved paths for Kubernetes, reproducible infrastructure, fast CI/CD, and honest SLOs.

What you will do
• Design and operate Kubernetes clusters on EKS; manage Helm charts, cluster upgrades, pod autoscaling, network policies, and secrets.
• Provision and evolve cloud infrastructure with Terraform; enforce reproducibility and drift detection in all environments.
• Own CI/CD pipelines in GitHub Actions: build, test, promote artefacts across dev, staging, and production with safe rollback procedures.
• Define and track SLOs with Prometheus and Grafana; participate in incident response, write blameless postmortems, and follow through on reliability items.
• Guide application teams on containerisation, observability instrumentation, and cost-aware resource requests.

What we look for
• 4+ years in DevOps, SRE, or platform engineering.
• Deep Linux and Docker; strong Kubernetes operational experience.
• Terraform as primary IaC tool; confident with AWS (VPC, IAM, EKS, RDS, S3).
• GitHub Actions or equivalent CI/CD; deployment strategies (blue/green, canary).

Nice to have
• Ansible for configuration management.
• GitLab CI or CircleCI experience for pipeline migrations.
• Datadog or Splunk for observability at larger scale.
• Policy-as-code (OPA/Gatekeeper) or SOC 2 compliance patterns.
financial analyst:
Financial Analyst — FP&A

You will support forecasting, budgeting, and management reporting for operational leaders. Models must be auditable, variance commentary credible, and deliverables on time during month-end and planning cycles.

What you will do
• Build and maintain rolling forecasts, annual budgets, and long-range plans in Excel; improve template structure, controls, and version discipline.
• Perform monthly and quarterly variance analysis (actuals vs plan vs forecast); write commentary for leadership packs and board materials.
• Partner with business units on headcount, opex, and capital requests; help them model options and understand trade-offs.
• Support audit and internal control documentation; ensure data reconciles from source systems to management reports.
• Build Power BI dashboards for self-serve financial visibility where stakeholders need it.

What we look for
• 3+ years in corporate finance, FP&A, or closely related financial analysis.
• Advanced Excel: complex modelling, pivot tables, dynamic references, and auditability discipline.
• Strong grasp of P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow fundamentals.
• Clear writing and comfort presenting variance explanations to finance and non-finance leaders.

Nice to have
• SQL for pulling data from financial data warehouses without waiting on IT.
• Tableau for data-heavy visualisation needs beyond Power BI.
• Experience in technology, retail, or manufacturing cost structures.
• Exposure to SAP, Oracle, or Workday as data sources.
frontend developer:
Senior Frontend Engineer

You will build accessible, performant web applications used daily by customers and internal teams, working closely with product designers (Figma) and backend engineers (OpenAPI/REST contracts).

What you will do
• Implement features in React and TypeScript with semantic HTML, keyboard navigation, and WCAG accessibility as first-class requirements.
• Use Next.js for server-side rendering and static generation where performance and SEO matter; Vite for SPA builds.
• Apply Tailwind CSS to a shared component system; contribute to and enforce a Storybook component library.
• Write unit tests with Jest or Vitest and end-to-end tests with Playwright; maintain CI coverage gates in GitHub Actions.
• Collaborate with backend on REST and OpenAPI contracts; review API designs for frontend usability.

What we look for
• 3+ years shipping production web applications.
• Deep JavaScript and TypeScript; expert command of HTML and CSS.
• Strong React experience including hooks, context, and component composition patterns.
• Experience integrating REST APIs; understanding of HTTP, auth flows, and error handling.

Nice to have
• Edge/CDN configuration and Core Web Vitals optimisation.
• Internationalisation pipelines.
• GraphQL client-side experience (Apollo or Relay).
• Visual regression testing in CI.
full stack developer:
Full Stack Engineer — Product Squad

You will carry features from React UI through a Node.js API to PostgreSQL, owning the full vertical slice. The team is small and cross-functional; pragmatic architecture and visible weekly progress matter.

What you will do
• Build user-facing features in React and TypeScript; implement responsive layouts and wire up API calls with clean error handling.
• Design and implement Node.js REST APIs; define data models and migrations in PostgreSQL; write integration tests alongside feature code.
• Maintain Docker-based local development and deploy through GitHub Actions to AWS; own basic operational concerns (logging, health checks, feature flags).
• Participate in product discovery with PMs and designers; break work into shippable increments and demo weekly.
• Review teammate code for correctness, maintainability, and security (auth, input validation, SQL injection).

What we look for
• 3+ years of full stack experience with JavaScript or TypeScript on both frontend and backend.
• PostgreSQL: schema design, indexing, and migrations using an ORM or raw SQL.
• Git workflow discipline; comfort in agile ceremonies.
• OAuth 2.0 integration patterns and REST API design fundamentals.

Nice to have
• GraphQL for API teams moving toward a BFF layer.
• MongoDB for document-centric data alongside relational data.
• Next.js for SSR/ISR pages where SEO matters.
• Python for scripting or data pipeline tasks.
hr business partner:
HR Business Partner

You will partner with people managers across an engineering and product organisation, supporting performance, employee relations, and talent decisions with both empathy and policy rigour.

What you will do
• Advise managers on performance improvement plans, promotions, and compensation cycle inputs; facilitate calibration sessions.
• Handle employee relations matters — investigations, disciplinary processes, separations — with discretion and appropriate documentation.
• Support quarterly talent reviews; identify flight risks, high potentials, and succession gaps in collaboration with HR CoEs.
• Improve onboarding quality and engagement initiatives for your client groups; act on eNPS and exit interview themes.
• Partner with talent acquisition on role scoping and offer approval for critical hires.

What we look for
• 5+ years in an HRBP or strategic HR generalist role.
• Strong judgment and communication across all levels, from individual contributor to VP.
• Working knowledge of employment law fundamentals; ability to escalate appropriately.
• Experience using an HRIS (Workday, BambooHR, or equivalent) and comfort with people metrics.

Nice to have
• Experience supporting engineering, R&D, or global capability centre populations.
• Data literacy for workforce planning and attrition modelling.
• Exposure to M&A people integration or rapid org scaling contexts.
ml engineer:
Machine Learning Engineer — Production ML

You will productionise models built by data scientists: reproducible training pipelines, scalable batch and online inference, monitoring, and safe rollouts. Latency, cost, and failure isolation are first-class requirements.

What you will do
• Build and maintain batch training and scoring pipelines in Python, orchestrated by Airflow, running on containerised compute.
• Package models with Docker, deploy to Kubernetes, and implement REST inference endpoints with health checks and graceful degradation.
• Implement drift detection, data validation, and alerting with MLflow; define triggers for retraining cycles.
• Work with data scientists on reproducibility: environment pinning, dataset versioning, and experiment tracking.
• Secure model artefacts, feature pipelines, and data access paths; write runbooks for on-call incidents.

What we look for
• 3+ years in ML engineering or backend engineering with significant ML production ownership.
• Python proficiency; PyTorch for deep learning models; scikit-learn for classical models.
• Docker and Kubernetes operational experience; REST API design for model serving.
• SQL and cloud fundamentals on AWS or GCP.

Nice to have
• TensorFlow or JAX for teams with legacy model code.
• Real-time inference (Kafka, feature stores, Triton) for low-latency SLAs.
• GPU cluster management or spot-instance training cost optimisation.
• SageMaker, Vertex AI, or Azure ML as a managed training platform.
mobile developer:
Mobile Engineer — Cross-Platform

You will ship and maintain a consumer mobile application in React Native (TypeScript), targeting both iOS and Android from a single codebase with store-quality stability and performance.

What you will do
• Implement product features end-to-end: UI components, navigation, local storage, REST API integration, push notifications, and deep links.
• Profile and fix rendering jank, memory leaks, JS thread blocking, and battery regressions; use Crashlytics or Sentry in production.
• Participate in release trains and staged rollouts via Fastlane or GitHub Actions; manage hotfix procedures for critical issues.
• Collaborate with backend engineers on API contract design, versioning, and error handling for mobile-specific latency constraints.
• Maintain Firebase integrations (Analytics, Remote Config, FCM) and App Store / Google Play release metadata.

What we look for
• 3+ years mobile development with apps published to App Store and/or Google Play.
• Strong React Native and TypeScript; understanding of the bridge and new architecture (JSI/Fabric) is a plus.
• Git discipline and experience with automated CI for mobile builds and tests.

Nice to have
• Flutter/Dart if the team evaluates a cross-platform migration.
• Swift or Kotlin for native module development when React Native cannot cover a platform API.
• Detox or Maestro for end-to-end mobile test automation.
performance test engineer:
Performance Test Engineer

You will translate non-functional requirements into executable load, stress, and soak scenarios and deliver findings that engineering and architecture teams can act on. Credibility of methodology — realistic workloads, clean environments, honest baselines — matters as much as the numbers themselves.

What you will do
• Design workload models from production traffic patterns or capacity forecast inputs; script scenarios in JMeter (primary) or k6 for teams preferring a code-first tool.
• Execute tests in representative environments; capture latency percentiles, throughput, and error budgets; correlate with Grafana and Prometheus dashboards.
• Isolate bottlenecks — application logic, database queries, connection pools, caching layers, third-party APIs — and document findings with reproduction steps.
• Support capacity planning discussions before major launches or traffic-spike events.
• Write clear test reports that communicate pass/fail against NFR thresholds.

What we look for
• 4+ years dedicated performance or reliability testing experience.
• Strong HTTP and REST API protocol knowledge; experience testing multi-tier web applications.
• Linux familiarity for server-side profiling; SQL for database timing analysis.
• Python or Java for scripting custom scenarios and result processing.

Nice to have
• Datadog APM or New Relic for application-level profiling.
• Cloud-scale load generation (AWS, k6 Cloud, or Gatling Enterprise).
• Chaos engineering basics (Gremlin, LitmusChaos).
product manager:
Product Manager — B2B SaaS

You will own outcomes for a product area: discovery, roadmap, prioritisation, and delivery with engineering and design. We expect crisp written communication, data-informed tradeoffs, and accountability against OKRs — not just shipping features.

What you will do
• Run continuous discovery with customers and stakeholders; synthesise problems into prioritised hypotheses with measurable success criteria.
• Maintain a public roadmap and sprint backlog in Jira; write one-pagers or PRDs that give engineering enough context to make good decisions.
• Partner with engineering on scope, technical constraints, and incremental release strategies; with design on flows, edge cases, and usability.
• Analyse funnel data, experiment results, and support patterns; identify what the data says and what it does not.
• Work with GTM on positioning, sales enablement, and launch planning for significant releases.

What we look for
• 4+ years as a product manager on software products with measurable business outcomes.
• Excellent writing; you can communicate trade-offs and say no with clarity.
• SQL proficiency or self-serve analytics tools (Amplitude, Mixpanel, Looker) for independent insight generation.
• Agile delivery experience; understand engineering well enough for honest scope conversations.

Nice to have
• Technical background or exposure to API and data platform products.
• Enterprise sales-motion experience.
• Figma for light wireframing and design collaboration.
qa engineer:
QA Engineer — Quality and Automation

You will ensure releases meet agreed quality standards through risk-based test planning, hands-on execution, and automation that is worth its maintenance cost. Working with developers before code reaches review is expected, not optional.

What you will do
• Author test plans and test cases; execute functional, regression, and exploratory testing across web applications and REST APIs.
• Build and maintain end-to-end UI automation with Playwright — the team's primary framework. Cypress is an accepted alternative if that is your background.
• Write API tests with Postman or a scripted equivalent; integrate suites into the CI pipeline with clear pass/fail gates.
• Manage defect lifecycle in Jira: reproduce, document, triage with developers, and verify fixes.
• Collaborate with business analysts on acceptance criteria before development starts; support UAT with stakeholders.

What we look for
• 3+ years combining manual and automated testing on web or API products.
• Solid Playwright or Cypress automation skills; able to maintain and refactor a test suite, not just record scripts.
• SQL basics for data verification; Git and CI/CD familiarity.
• Agile delivery experience; quality is a shared responsibility with developers.

Nice to have
• Selenium experience for migrating legacy suites to modern frameworks.
• BDD with Cucumber or Gherkin for behaviour-driven teams.
• Basic performance or mobile testing exposure.
salesforce developer:
Salesforce Developer

You will implement and maintain features on a Salesforce Sales Cloud org: Apex business logic, Lightning Web Components, declarative automation, and REST integrations with external systems.

What you will do
• Develop Apex classes and triggers; build Lightning Web Components for custom UI requirements; implement Flows and validation rules for declarative automation.
• Design SOQL queries, custom objects, and object relationships that respect governor limits and the Salesforce data model.
• Build REST integrations: outbound callouts to external APIs and inbound connected app endpoints, with proper error handling and named credentials.
• Manage metadata deployments using SFDX or change sets; participate in code review, static analysis, and release governance.
• Partner with business analysts and admins on requirements; support UAT and hypercare for new functionality.

What we look for
• 3+ years Salesforce development with production Sales Cloud org experience.
• Strong Apex and LWC; comfortable mixing declarative and programmatic approaches.
• Understanding of the Salesforce security model: profiles, permission sets, sharing rules, and field-level security.
• Git-based workflow for metadata management.

Nice to have
• Service Cloud, Experience Cloud, or CPQ configuration experience.
• MuleSoft or middleware integration patterns.
• Salesforce Platform Developer or App Builder certification.
sap consultant:
SAP Functional Consultant

You will deliver SAP implementation and enhancement work across design, configuration, and testing. Workshops are structured; documentation must meet audit standards; and offshore/onsite coordination needs to stay disciplined across the project lifecycle.

What you will do
• Lead or support fit-gap workshops for your functional module; produce configuration decisions, process documentation, and BRD or FRD updates.
• Configure assigned SAP modules; execute unit tests and participate in integration and regression testing cycles.
• Plan and execute UAT: write test scripts, coordinate business users, document defects, and track resolution through hypercare.
• Manage data migration activities within your module scope: mapping templates, cleansing rules, and mock load validation.
• Coordinate with technical, basis, and integration teams; maintain traceability between requirements and test evidence.

What we look for
• 4+ years SAP functional experience in at least one module (FI/CO, MM/SD, or PP) across a full project lifecycle.
• Structured documentation: configuration guides, test scripts, and training materials to an auditable standard.
• Excel and SQL for data analysis, migration templates, and reconciliation.
• Strong stakeholder communication across business owners and project managers.

Nice to have
• S/4HANA migration or greenfield implementation experience.
• ABAP debugging for understanding technical root causes.
• Integration touchpoints with Salesforce, ServiceNow, or middleware platforms.
sdet:
Software Development Engineer in Test

You will build test infrastructure and automation as production-grade software: reusable frameworks, reliable CI gates, and a relentless focus on reducing flaky tests and improving developer feedback loops.

What you will do
• Design and implement test frameworks in Java; cover UI flows with Playwright and REST APIs with REST Assured or an equivalent library.
• Integrate test suites into GitHub Actions CI with fast, parallelised feedback; define quality gates without creating artificial bottlenecks.
• Partner with developers on testability: mocking strategies, contract tests, and data seeding patterns that make tests deterministic.
• Track and reduce intermittent failures with metrics; own the flaky-test triage process and enforce a zero-tolerance policy on unaddressed flakiness.
• Review application code for missing test hooks and contribute to coding standards that make the system verifiable.

What we look for
• 3+ years with strong software engineering skills applied to quality infrastructure.
• Java as primary automation language; comfortable reading and contributing to application code.
• Playwright for modern UI automation; understanding of browser DevTools and network interception.
• Git, GitHub Actions, and Docker in a team CI context.

Nice to have
• Python for exploratory scripts or data-driven test generation.
• Selenium for migrating legacy suite assets.
• Kubernetes-based ephemeral test environments.
• BDD with Cucumber for behaviour-specification-aligned teams.
servicenow developer:
ServiceNow Developer

You will build and maintain ITSM workflows, catalogue items, and integrations on ServiceNow — writing maintainable code, keeping the CMDB clean, and testing rigorously before upgrades.

What you will do
• Develop server-side scripts (GlideScript/JavaScript), business rules, script includes, and Flow Designer flows for incident, change, and request processes.
• Build and maintain REST integrations between ServiceNow and external systems; use MID Server or Integration Hub where appropriate.
• Design catalogue items and record producers aligned to ITIL process intent; maintain CMDB data quality through scheduled discovery and manual audits.
• Plan and execute update set or DevOps pipeline promotions; test in sub-production instances before production deployment; document runbooks for upgrade readiness.
• Collaborate with process owners on requirements; translate ITIL process intent into platform configuration without over-customising.

What we look for
• 3+ years ServiceNow development on ITSM (Incident, Change, Problem, CMDB as a minimum).
• JavaScript proficiency; REST API understanding (both consuming and publishing).
• Agile delivery with Jira or equivalent; manage work items and communicate status.

Nice to have
• ITIL Foundation certification.
• HRSD, CSM, or ITOM module experience.
• Automated testing using ATF (Automated Test Framework) for upgrade protection.
snowflake engineer:
Snowflake Data Engineer

You will own the Snowflake data warehouse layer end-to-end: architecture decisions, RBAC, performance, cost governance, and the dbt-powered transformation layer that BI and analytics teams consume.

What you will do
• Design and maintain Snowflake objects — databases, schemas, virtual warehouses, roles, and row-access policies — with security and cost efficiency in mind.
• Build and manage dbt models: layered transformations (staging, intermediate, mart), data tests, documentation, and exposure to BI consumers.
• Orchestrate pipeline runs with Airflow; define SLAs, handle backfills cleanly, and alert on quality failures before they reach dashboards.
• Ingest data from AWS S3 and other cloud sources using Snowpipe, COPY INTO, or ELT patterns; optimise clustering and micro-partitioning for query workloads.
• Partner with Tableau and Power BI consumers on semantic layer design; fix slow dashboard queries at source.

What we look for
• 3+ years with Snowflake in a production engineering role (not just analyst use).
• Expert SQL: window functions, semi-structured data (VARIANT, PARSE_JSON), and performance tuning with QUERY_HISTORY.
• Hands-on dbt and Airflow in a team environment.
• AWS fundamentals; IAM, S3 lifecycle, and data transfer costs.

Nice to have
• Azure Synapse or BigQuery in multi-cloud environments.
• Kafka or Kinesis for streaming ingestion into Snowpipe.
• Apache Spark for workloads that exceed Snowflake native compute.
• Databricks familiarity for mixed-stack organisations.
software engineer:
Software Engineer

You will design, build, and operate distributed services that power core product experiences. We value ownership, operational excellence, and customer impact — on-call is shared, load is managed, and postmortems lead to real changes.

What you will do
• Deliver features across the service lifecycle: design doc, implementation, tests, staged rollout, and post-release monitoring.
• Define SLOs and error budgets for your services; build actionable alerts and runbooks so the team can respond confidently at any hour.
• Ship through GitHub Actions CI/CD; participate in code review with genuine feedback; mentor peers on production patterns and engineering judgement.
• Partner with product and design in discovery; apply security and privacy practices (auth, input validation, secrets management) from the start, not as an afterthought.
• Contribute to architectural decisions; write design documents that communicate trade-offs, not just conclusions.

What we look for
• 3+ years building production distributed systems in Java or Go.
• Strong computer science fundamentals: data structures, concurrency, system design.
• PostgreSQL: schema design, query tuning, and understanding of MVCC and index strategies.
• REST API design; Docker and Kubernetes operational experience; GitHub Actions or equivalent CI/CD.
• One major cloud deeply (AWS or GCP): networking, IAM, managed services, and cost awareness.

Nice to have
• Python for tooling, data pipeline work, or scripting-heavy services.
• gRPC for high-performance internal service communication.
• Kafka or another event streaming platform.
• Multi-region deployment and disaster recovery patterns.
technical recruiter:
Technical Recruiter — Engineering Talent

You will own full-cycle hiring for software engineering and adjacent technical roles: sourcing, screening, interview coordination, and offer support. Hiring managers expect thorough preparation, calibrated feedback, and a candidate experience that reflects the company well.

What you will do
• Partner with hiring managers to define role requirements, build structured interview plans, and align on assessment criteria before the first candidate is screened.
• Source and engage passive candidates through LinkedIn Recruiter, Boolean search, and community channels; represent the employer value proposition honestly.
• Coordinate interview loops end-to-end: scheduling, prep notes for interviewers, structured debrief facilitation, and timely candidate communication.
• Maintain clean ATS records (Greenhouse, Lever, or equivalent); track pipeline metrics and share conversion data to identify process improvements.
• Support offer construction and negotiation through to acceptance.

What we look for
• 3+ years recruiting software engineers or closely adjacent technical roles.
• Strong organisation and follow-through across multiple open roles simultaneously.
• Clear, honest communication with candidates and hiring managers at every stage.
• Comfort discussing technical role requirements and screening for basic competency signals.

Nice to have
• Campus or early-career programme experience.
• Employer branding contributions (content, events, community).
• Data fluency: building pipeline dashboards or analysing sourcing channel effectiveness.
test lead:
Test Lead / QA Lead

You will own quality for a programme or product line: test strategy, estimation, execution oversight, and upward communication. You are accountable for traceability, risk-based coverage, and the growth of testers on the team.

What you will do
• Define and own the test strategy and master test plan; estimate effort and resource allocation for each release cycle.
• Maintain the Requirements Traceability Matrix; enforce entry and exit criteria; run release readiness reviews with project managers and business owners.
• Oversee manual and automated execution; triage defects, escalate blockers, and run status calls with stakeholders.
• Coach testers on tool selection, automation approach, defect writing quality, and risk identification.
• Drive root-cause analysis on escaped defects; feed lessons back into process and coverage improvements.

What we look for
• 6+ years QA experience, including at least 2 years leading or coordinating a test team.
• Test planning, estimation, and RTM discipline in Jira or equivalent.
• Agile and waterfall delivery awareness; adapt quality gates to the delivery model in use.
• Clear written and verbal communication; summarise quality risk credibly to non-technical stakeholders.

Nice to have
• Automation oversight: reviewing framework design and CI integration without writing all the code.
• Domain experience in BFSI, ERP, or regulated-industry testing.
• ISTQB Advanced Test Manager or equivalent certification.
